title: Common Mistakes — Basics of English
subtitle: Hindi speakers ki sabse common English galtiyan — aur unke sahi solutions
description: Ye sab mistakes Hindi speakers karte hain. Har ek ko dhyan se padho aur yaad rakho. Practice mein inhe avoid karo.

mistakes.explanation: He/She/It ke saath 'doesn't' use hota hai, 'don't' nahi
She ke saath 'doesn't'
He ke saath verb+s lagata hai (goes)
She ke saath 'has' (have ka special form)
Doesn't ke baad base verb (eat, not eats)
They ke saath verb mein 's' NAHI lagata
We ke saath base verb

mistakes.wrong: I am working here since 5 years.
mistakes.correct: I have been working here for 5 years.
mistakes.explanation: Duration ke liye 'for', point of time ke liye 'since'. Present Perfect Continuous use hota hai.
Collective nouns (team, group, family) ke saath singular verb
Each/Every ke saath singular noun

mistakes.wrong: Me and Ram went to market.
mistakes.correct: Ram and I went to market.
mistakes.explanation: Subject position mein 'I' use karo, 'Me' nahi. Aur pehle doosre ka naam lo!

mistakes.wrong: Him and her went to school.
mistakes.correct: He and she went to school.
mistakes.explanation: Subject position mein subject pronouns (He, She)
Preposition ke baad object pronoun use hota hai (me, not I)
I ke saath hamesha 'am' use hota hai, 'is' nahi

mistakes.wrong: This is his bag. I like it's color.
mistakes.correct: This is his bag. I like its color.
mistakes.explanation: 'Its' = possession (kisi cheez ka). 'It's' = it is (short form)
Verb ke baad object pronoun (us, not we)
Profession ke pehle 'a' ya 'an' zaroor lagao. 'engineer' vowel se shuru = 'an'
Singular countable noun ke pehle article zaroor lagao
Specific well-known places ke pehle 'the' lagao

mistakes.wrong: The water is important for health.
mistakes.explanation: General statements mein uncountable nouns ke pehle 'the' nahi lagata
Sports ke pehle article nahi lagata
Languages ke pehle 'the' nahi lagata

mistakes.wrong: I am feeling happy. The happiness is important.
mistakes.explanation: General/abstract nouns (general meaning mein) ke pehle 'the' nahi
Superlative adjectives ke pehle 'the' zaroori hai
Singular countable noun = 'a' or 'an' zaroori
'University' = 'yu' sound se shuru hota hai (consonant sound) = 'a' use karo

mistakes.correct: I have been here since morning.
mistakes.explanation: 'Since' ke saath Perfect tense use hota hai

mistakes.correct: He has been tired since yesterday.
mistakes.explanation: 'Since' ke saath Present Perfect ya Perfect Continuous

mistakes.wrong: I am knowing him for 5 years.
mistakes.correct: I have known him for 5 years.
mistakes.explanation: 'Know', 'love', 'like', 'understand' — ye stative verbs hain, inhe '-ing' form mein use nahi karte
'Have' = possession ke liye — continuous form mein nahi
Stative verb 'want' — continuous form nahi
Yesterday ke saath past tense (V2 form)
Am/Is/Are ke baad V4 (-ing) form use karo
Is/Am/Are ke baad verb+-ing form use karo
'Go' ka past tense 'went' hai (irregular verb)
'Have/Has' ke baad V3 (Past Participle) form — 'eaten' not 'ate'
'Daily', 'always', 'usually' jaise adverbs ki position dhyan se rakho
English mein SVO order — verb pehle, adjective baad mein with 'is'
Adjectives ka order: Size → Color → Noun
English mein 'want someone to do' pattern use hota hai
Question mein Do/Does zaroor lagao
WH question mein does + base verb ka order
Frequency adverbs 'is/am/are' ke BAAD aate hain
'Married to' — 'with' nahi
Skills ke liye 'good at'
'Afraid of' — 'from' nahi

mistakes.wrong: I am bored of this work.
mistakes.correct: I am bored with this work.
mistakes.explanation: 'Bored with' ya 'bored by' — 'of' nahi (British English mein 'of' chalti hai but 'with' better hai)

mistakes.wrong: He is interested in to learn English.
mistakes.correct: He is interested in learning English.
mistakes.explanation: Preposition ke baad V4 (-ing) form
'Discuss' already 'about' ka meaning contain karta hai — 'about' mat lagao
'Reach' ke baad 'to' nahi lagata (direct object)
'Have a fever' = standard English. 'Having' yahan use nahi hota

mistakes.wrong: My name is Ram. What is your good name?
mistakes.correct: My name is Ram. What is your name?
mistakes.explanation: Hindi mein 'shubh naam' bolte hain but English mein simply 'name' — 'good name' sirf Indian English mein
'Cousin' already brother ya sister include karta hai — 'sister' ya 'brother' extra mat lagao

mistakes.wrong: I will call you on your number.
mistakes.explanation: 'Call' ka matlab hi phone karna hai — 'on your number' extra hai

mistakes.correct: Let me suggest something. / Here is an idea.
mistakes.explanation: 'Do one thing' direct Hindi translation hai — English mein aise nahi bolte

mistakes.correct: I am Ram Kumar. / My name is Ram Kumar.
mistakes.explanation: 'Myself' = apna introduction ka yeh tarika Hinglish hai — formal English mein 'I am' use karo
Hindi mein 'hi' lagaate hain (sirf Delhi se hi hoon) but English mein 'only' yahan fit nahi hota

mistakes.wrong: He was not present because he was ill from yesterday.
mistakes.correct: He was not present because he had been ill since yesterday.
mistakes.explanation: 'Since' ke saath perfect tense, aur 'ill from yesterday' nahi bolte
'Only' ka Hinglish influence — 'right here' better hai
Hindi passive (aa gaya) ko English mein present perfect active se express karo
Countable noun ke pehle article zaroor lagao

mistakes.correct: He is out of town. / He is traveling.
mistakes.explanation: 'Out of station' sirf Indian English mein — standard mein 'out of town' ya 'traveling' bolte hain

mistakes.wrong: I will revert back to you.
mistakes.correct: I will get back to you.
mistakes.explanation: 'Revert' already 'back' include karta hai — 'revert back' redundant hai

topPriority: He/She/It ke saath ALWAYS use doesn't (not don't)
Subject position mein 'I' (not 'Me') — Ram and I went (not me and Ram)
Profession ke pehle article: I am AN engineer
Stative verbs (know, want, love, have) ko continuous mein mat use karo
Sports, languages, meals ke pehle 'the' mat lagao
